reasoning: The stock closed at 50.72 on 2025-03-28. The historical trend from the last quarter of 2024 to Q1 2025 is slightly negative, giving a P(>50.72) of 0.48 for the very short-term (April 6) by a simple linear model. However, on the fundamental side, a $4B buyback plan was recently authorized, and the board seems eager to deploy it, having repurchased shares extensively in early 2025 while the stock trades around $50 (market cap ~15B, so buyback is huge). The stock has been trading near its 52-week lows, with Wall Street price targets around $67 (suggesting upside). For April 6, only a week out, the probability should be close to 50% but heavily weighted by the short-term negative momentum, perhaps slightly countered by the buyback floor — leading to ~0.53. As we look at April 29, June, September, and eventually March 2026, the ongoing buyback, possible earnings beats (Q1 reported in May), and reversion to the mean from the 52-week low will provide an upward drift. Equity markets drift upwards typically over multi-quarter horizons. Therefore, I incrementally increase the probability from 0.53 for the short-term to 0.59 for the long-term horizons, as uncertainty increases but leans favorably due to robust capital return.
